sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 27

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 27:1
items.html:
(to the question) Dormita [slumber]
After the Partzuf rises, in the secret of MAN [Mayin Nukvin], to its upper one — when the Mochin in it depart and come to the upper one — the Partzuf is discerned as remaining below in a state of Dormita, which means sleep. This is because the Mochin did not depart from it entirely, but left in it a little vitality, in the measure of a person asleep, who is not considered entirely dead, for a certain measure of vitality is still in him. (p. 808, Item 106; and p. 810, Item 108)
